title: narcissus poeticus var. radiiflorus
intro: Narcissus poeticus var. radiiflorus, commonly known as the Pheasant's Eye or Radiated Narcissus, is a variety of the Pheasant's Eye daffodil that produces white flowers with bright yellow radiating petals. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Narcissus poeticus var. radiiflorus include treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as having antiseptic and antifungal properties that can aid in wound healing. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which are often used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
